NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the command of God our Savior and of Christ Jesus our hope,
text.Rotherham: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus—by injunction of God our Saviour and Christ Jesus our hope,—

text.NRSV: To Timothy, my loyal child in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: unto Timothy, my true child in faith: favour, mercy, peace, from God our Father, and Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: I urge you, as I did when I was on my way to Macedonia, to remain in Ephesus so that you may instruct certain people not to teach any different doctrine,
text.Rotherham: Even as I exhorted thee to remain in Ephesus, when I was journeying into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some—

text.NRSV: and not to occupy themselves with myths and endless genealogies that promote speculations rather than the divine training that is known by faith.
text.Rotherham: Not to be teaching otherwise, nor yet to be giving heed to stories and endless genealogies,—the which, bring, arguings, rather than that stewardship of God which is with faith;—

text.NRSV: But the aim of such instruction is love that comes from a pure heart, a good conscience, and sincere faith.
text.Rotherham: Now, the end of the charge, is love—out of a pure heart, and a good conscience, and faith unfeigned,—

text.NRSV: Some people have deviated from these and turned to meaningless talk,
text.Rotherham: Which some, missing, have turned them aside unto idle talk,

text.NRSV: desiring to be teachers of the law, without understanding either what they are saying or the things about which they make confident assertions.
text.Rotherham: Desiring to be law-teachers,—not understanding, either what they say or whereof they confidently affirm.

text.NRSV: Now we know that the law is good, if one uses it legitimately.
text.Rotherham: Now we know that, excellent, is the law, if one put it to a lawful use:—

text.NRSV: This means understanding that the law is laid down not for the innocent but for the lawless and disobedient, for the godless and sinful, for the unholy and profane, for those who kill their father or mother, for murderers,
text.Rotherham: Knowing this—that, to a righteous man, law, doth not apply, but to the lawless and insubordinate, ungodly and sinful, irreligious and profane, smiters of fathers and smiters of mothers, murderers,

text.NRSV: fornicators, sodomites, slave traders, liars, perjurers, and whatever else is contrary to the sound teaching
text.Rotherham: fornicators, sodomites, man-stealers, liars, false- swearers,—and, if anything else, unto the healthful teaching, is opposed;—

text.NRSV: that conforms to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which he entrusted to me.
text.Rotherham: According to the glad-message of the glory of the happy God, with which entrusted am, I.

text.NRSV: I am grateful to Christ Jesus our Lord, who has strengthened me, because he judged me faithful and appointed me to his service,
text.Rotherham: Grateful, am I unto him that empowered me, Christ Jesus our Lord, in that, faithful, he accounted me, putting me into ministry,—

text.NRSV: even though I was formerly a blasphemer, a persecutor, and a man of violence. But I received mercy because I had acted ignorantly in unbelief,
text.Rotherham: Though, formerly, a defamer, and persecutor, and insulter; nevertheless mercy was shown me, because, without knowledge, I acted, in unbelief:

text.NRSV: and the grace of our Lord overflowed for me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Yet exceeding abundant was the favour of our Lord, with faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners—of whom I am the foremost.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying! and, of all acceptance, worthy,—that, Christ Jesus, came into the world, sinners, to save: of whom, the chief, am, I;—

text.NRSV: But for that very reason I received mercy, so that in me, as the foremost, Jesus Christ might display the utmost patience, making me an example to those who would come to believe in him for eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, on this account, was mercy shewn me,—that, in me, the chief, Christ Jesus might shew forth his entire longsuffering, for an ensample of them about to believe on him unto life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: To the King of the ages, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Now, unto the King of the ages,—incorruptible, invisible, alone God, be honour and glory, unto the ages of ages, Amen!

text.NRSV: I am giving you these instructions, Timothy, my child, in accordance with the prophecies made earlier concerning you, so that by following them you may fight the good fight,
text.Rotherham: This charge, I commit unto thee, child Timothy, According to the prophecies, running before on thee, in order that thou mightest war, with them, the noble warfare.

text.NRSV: having faith and a good conscience. By rejecting conscience, certain persons have suffered shipwreck in the faith;
text.Rotherham: Holding faith and a good conscience,—which some, thrusting from them, concerning their faith, have made shipwreck:—

text.NRSV: among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have turned over to Satan, so that they may learn not to blaspheme.
text.Rotherham: Of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may be taught by discipline not to be defaming.

text.NRSV: First of all, then, I urge that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for everyone,
text.Rotherham: I exhort, therefore, first of all, that there be made—supplications, prayers, intercessions, thankgivings, in behalf of all men,

text.NRSV: for kings and all who are in high positions, so that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and dignity.
text.Rotherham: In behalf of kings, and all them who are, in eminent station; in order that, an undisturbed, and quiet life, we may lead, in all godliness and gravity:

text.NRSV: This is right and is acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
text.Rotherham: This, is comely and acceptable before our Saviour God,

text.NRSV: who desires everyone to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
text.Rotherham: Who willeth, all men, to be saved, and, unto a personal knowledge of truth, to come;

text.NRSV: For there is one God; there is also one mediator between God and humankind, Christ Jesus, himself human,
text.Rotherham: For there is, one, God, one, mediator also, between God and men,—a man—Christ Jesus:

text.NRSV: who gave himself a ransom for all—this was attested at the right time.
text.Rotherham: Who gave himself a ransom in behalf of all,—the testimony, in its own fit times:

text.NRSV: For this I was appointed a herald and an apostle (I am telling the truth, I am not lying), a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
text.Rotherham: Unto which, I, have been appointed proclaimer and apostle—Truth I speak, I utter no falsehood—a teacher of nations, in faith and truth.

text.NRSV: I desire, then, that in every place the men should pray, lifting up holy hands without anger or argument;
text.Rotherham: I am minded, therefore, that—the men in every place be offering prayer, uplifting hands of lovingkindness, apart from anger and disputings;

text.NRSV: also that the women should dress themselves modestly and decently in suitable clothing, not with hair braided with gold or pearls or expensive clothes,
text.Rotherham: In the same way, that, the women, in seemly attire, with modesty and sober-mindedness, be adorning themselves,—not with plaitings and ornamentation of gold, or with pearls, or with costly apparel,—

text.NRSV: but with good works, as is proper for women who profess reverence for God.
text.Rotherham: But, which becometh women promising godliness, through means of good works.

text.NRSV: Let a woman learn in silence with full submission.
text.Rotherham: Let, a woman, in quietness, be learning in all submission;

text.NRSV: I permit no woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she is to keep silent.
text.Rotherham: But, teaching—unto a woman, I do not permit, nor yet to have authority over a man,—but to be in quietness;

text.NRSV: For Adam was formed first, then Eve;
text.Rotherham: For, Adam, first was formed, then Eve,

text.NRSV: and Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and became a transgressor.
text.Rotherham: And, Adam, was not deceived, whereas, the woman, having been wholly deceived, hath come to be, in transgression;

text.NRSV: Yet she will be saved through childbearing, provided they continue in faith and love and holiness, with modesty.
text.Rotherham: She shall be saved, however, through means of the child-bearing,—if they abide in faith, and love, and holiness, with sobermindedness. Faithful, the saying.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure: whoever aspires to the office of bishop desires a noble task.
text.Rotherham: If anyone, for oversight, is eager, a noble work, doth he covet:—

text.NRSV: Now a bishop must be above reproach, married only once, temperate, sensible, respectable, hospitable, an apt teacher,
text.Rotherham: It is needful, then, for, the overseer, to be irreproachable, a husband, of one wife, sober, of sound mind, orderly, hospitable, apt in teaching,

text.NRSV: not a drunkard, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, and not a lover of money.
text.Rotherham: Not given to wine, not ready to wound, but considerate, averse to contention, not fond of money,

text.NRSV: He must manage his own household well, keeping his children submissive and respectful in every way—
text.Rotherham: Over his own house, presiding, well, having, children, in submission, with all dignity;

text.NRSV: for if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how can he take care of God’s church?
text.Rotherham: Whereas, if anyone, over his own house, cannot, preside, how, of an assembly of God, shall he take care?

text.NRSV: He must not be a recent convert, or he may be puffed up with conceit and fall into the condemnation of the devil.
text.Rotherham: Not a new convert, lest, being beclouded, into the sentence of the adversary, he fall;

text.NRSV: Moreover, he must be well thought of by outsiders, so that he may not fall into disgrace and the snare of the devil.
text.Rotherham: It is needful, moreover, to have, an honourable testimony also, from them who are without, lest, into reproach, he fall, and the snare of the adversary.

text.NRSV: Deacons likewise must be serious, not double-tongued, not indulging in much wine, not greedy for money;
text.Rotherham: Ministers, in the same way,—dignified, not double-tongued, not, to much wine, given, not greedy of base gain,

text.NRSV: they must hold fast to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
text.Rotherham: Holding the sacred secret of the faith in a pure conscience;

text.NRSV: And let them first be tested; then, if they prove themselves blameless, let them serve as deacons.
text.Rotherham: But let, these also, be proved first, then, let them be ministering, being, unaccusable:

text.NRSV: Women likewise must be serious, not slanderers, but temperate, faithful in all things.
text.Rotherham: Wives, in the same way,—dignified, not given to intrigue, sober, faithful in all things.

text.NRSV: Let deacons be married only once, and let them manage their children and their households well;
text.Rotherham: Let, ministers, be husbands of, one wife, over children, presiding, well, and over their own houses;

text.NRSV: for those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: For, they who have ministered well, a good degree for themselves, are acquiring, and great freedom of speech in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: I hope to come to you soon, but I am writing these instructions to you so that,
text.Rotherham: These things, unto thee, I am writing, hoping to come [unto thee] shortly,—

text.NRSV: if I am delayed, you may know how one ought to behave in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and bulwark of the truth.
text.Rotherham: But, if I should tarry, that thou mayest know—how it behoveth, in a house of God, to behave oneself,—the which, is an assembly of a Living God, a pillar and basement of the truth;—

text.NRSV: Without any doubt, the mystery of our religion is great: He was revealed in flesh, vindicated in spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among Gentiles, believed in in the world, taken up in glory.
text.Rotherham: And, confessedly great, is the sacred secret of godliness,—Who was made manifest in flesh, was declared righteous in spirit, was made visible unto messengers, was proclaimed among nations, was believed on in the world, was taken up in glory.

text.NRSV: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will renounce the faith by paying attention to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons,
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the Spirit, expressly saith—that, in later seasons, some will revolt from the faith, giving heed unto seducing spirits, and unto teachings of demons—

text.NRSV: through the hypocrisy of liars whose consciences are seared with a hot iron.
text.Rotherham: in hypocrisy, speaking falsehood, [of demons] cauterised in their own conscience,—

text.NRSV: They forbid marriage and demand abstinence from foods, which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
text.Rotherham: Forbidding to marry, [commanding] to abstain from foods which, God, created to be received with thanksgiving by them who believe and personally know the truth;

text.NRSV: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, provided it is received with thanksgiving;
text.Rotherham: Because, every creature of God, is good, and nothing to be cast away, if, with thanksgiving, it be received,—

text.NRSV: for it is sanctified by God’s word and by prayer.
text.Rotherham: For it is hallowed by the word of God and intercession,

text.NRSV: If you put these instructions before the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound teaching that you have followed.
text.Rotherham: These things, submitting to the brethren, thou shall be, a noble, minister of Christ Jesus, nourishing thyself with the words of the faith, and of the noble teaching which thou hast closely studied.

text.NRSV: Have nothing to do with profane myths and old wives’ tales. Train yourself in godliness,
text.Rotherham: But, from the profane and old-wives’ stories, excuse thyself, and be training thyself unto godliness;

text.NRSV: for, while physical training is of some value, godliness is valuable in every way, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.
text.Rotherham: For, the bodily training, for little, is profitable, whereas, godliness, for all things is profitable,—having, promise, of life—the present and the coming.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure and worthy of full acceptance.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying! and, of all acceptance, worthy;

text.NRSV: For to this end we toil and struggle, because we have our hope set on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.
text.Rotherham: For, to this end, are we toiling and contending, because we have set our hope on a Living God, Who is Saviour of all men—specially, of such as believe.

text.NRSV: These are the things you must insist on and teach.
text.Rotherham: Be giving these things in charge, and be teaching:—

text.NRSV: Let no one despise your youth, but set the believers an example in speech and conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
text.Rotherham: Let, no one, despise, thy youth, but, an ensample, become thou of the faithful,—in discourse, in behaviour, in love, in faith, in chastity.

text.NRSV: Until I arrive, give attention to the public reading of scripture, to exhorting, to teaching.
text.Rotherham: While I am coming, be giving heed—to the reading, to the exhorting, to the teaching:

text.NRSV: Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you through prophecy with the laying on of hands by the council of elders.
text.Rotherham: Be not careless of the gift of favour, that is in thee, which was given thee through means of prophesying, along with a laying on of the hands of the eldership.

text.NRSV: Put these things into practice, devote yourself to them, so that all may see your progress.
text.Rotherham: These things, be thy care, in these things, be thou, that, thine advancement, may be, manifest, unto all:

text.NRSV: Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching; continue in these things, for in doing so you will save both yourself and your hearers.
text.Rotherham: Be giving heed to thyself, and to thy teaching,—abide still in them; for, this doing, both, thyself, shalt thou save, and them that hearken to thee.

text.NRSV: Do not speak harshly to an older man, but speak to him as to a father, to younger men as brothers,
text.Rotherham: An elderly man, do not thou reprimand, but beseech him, as [though he were thy] father,—younger men, as brothers,

text.NRSV: to older women as mothers, to younger women as sisters—with absolute purity.
text.Rotherham: Elderly women, as mothers, younger women, as sisters, in all chastity.

text.NRSV: Honor widows who are really widows.
text.Rotherham: Widows, honour thou—who indeed are widows:—

text.NRSV: If a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn their religious duty to their own family and make some repayment to their parents; for this is pleasing in God’s sight.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, if, any widow, hath, children or grandchildren, let them be learning—first, unto their own house, to be shewing reverence, and, returns, to be making unto their progenitors; for, this, is acceptable before God;

text.NRSV: The real widow, left alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day;
text.Rotherham: But, she who is indeed a widow, and is left alone, hath turned her hope towards God, and is giving attendance unto the supplications and the prayers, night and day,—

text.NRSV: but the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, she that runneth riot, while living, is, dead:

text.NRSV: Give these instructions as well, so that they may be above reproach.
text.Rotherham: And, these things, be giving in charge, that, without reproach, they may be;

text.NRSV: And whoever does not provide for relatives, and especially for family members, has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
text.Rotherham: If, however, anyone, for his own, and specially them of his household, taketh not forethought, his faith, hath he denied, and is worse, than one without faith!

text.NRSV: Let a widow be put on the list if she is not less than sixty years old and has been married only once;
text.Rotherham: Let, a widow, be put on the list—having become, not less than sixty years old, one man’s, wife,

text.NRSV: she must be well attested for her good works, as one who has brought up children, shown hospitality, washed the saints’ feet, helped the afflicted, and devoted herself to every good work.
text.Rotherham: In noble works, being well-attested;—if she hath nourished children, if she hath shewn hospitality, if, saints feet, she hath washed, if, them who were in tribulation, she hath succoured, if, in every good, work she hath followed on:

text.NRSV: But refuse to put younger widows on the list; for when their sensual desires alienate them from Christ, they want to marry,
text.Rotherham: But, younger widows, decline thou; for, as soon as they wax wanton against the Christ, to marry, are they determined,—

text.NRSV: and so they incur condemnation for having violated their first pledge.
text.Rotherham: Having, as sentence,—that, their first faith, they have set at nought;

text.NRSV: Besides that, they learn to be idle, gadding about from house to house; and they are not merely idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not say.
text.Rotherham: At the same time, to be idlers, are they learning, going about from house to house; and, not only idlers, but, gossips, also, and, busybodies,—saying the things they ought not,

text.NRSV: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, and manage their households, so as to give the adversary no occasion to revile us.
text.Rotherham: I am minded, therefore, that, the younger ones, marry, bear children, be mistress of the house,—giving, no single occasion, unto the opposer, as a cause, of reviling;

text.NRSV: For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
text.Rotherham: For, already, some have turned aside after Satan:

text.NRSV: If any believing woman has relatives who are widows, let her assist them; let the church not be burdened, so that it can assist those who are real widows.
text.Rotherham: If any, believing woman, hath widows, let her be giving them succour, and not suffer the assembly to be burdened,—that, them who are indeed widows, it may itself succour.

text.NRSV: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching;
text.Rotherham: Let, the well presiding elders, of double honour, be accounted worthy, especially they who toil in discourse and teaching;

text.NRSV: for the scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and, “The laborer deserves to be paid.”
text.Rotherham: For the scripture saith—A threshing ox, shalt thou not muzzle, and—Worthy, is the workman, of his hire.

text.NRSV: Never accept an accusation against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
text.Rotherham: Against an elder, entertain not, an accusation, except at, [the mouth of] two, or three, witnesses;

text.NRSV: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest also may stand in fear.
text.Rotherham: But, them who are sinning, before all, do thou reprove, that, the rest also, may have, fear:—

text.NRSV: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels, I warn you to keep these instructions without prejudice, doing nothing on the basis of partiality.
text.Rotherham: I adjure thee, before God, and Christ Jesus, and the chosen messengers, that, these things, thou observe, apart from prejudgment, doing, nothing, by partiality.

text.NRSV: Do not ordain anyone hastily, and do not participate in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
text.Rotherham: Hands, suddenly, upon no one, lay, neither have fellowship with sins of strangers: thyself, keep, chaste:—

text.NRSV: No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
text.Rotherham: No longer, be a water-drinker, but, of a little wine, make use, because of thy stomach and thy, frequent, sicknesses.

text.NRSV: The sins of some people are conspicuous and precede them to judgment, while the sins of others follow them there.
text.Rotherham: Some men’s sins, are, openly evident, leading on into judgment, with some, however, they even follow after;

text.NRSV: So also good works are conspicuous; and even when they are not, they cannot remain hidden.
text.Rotherham: In the same way, the noble works also, are openly evident,—and, they that are otherwise, cannot be hid.

text.NRSV: Let all who are under the yoke of slavery regard their masters as worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be blasphemed.
text.Rotherham: Let, as many as are servants under a yoke, be counting, their own masters, worthy, of all honour, lest, the name of God and the teaching, be defamed.

text.NRSV: Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful to them on the ground that they are members of the church; rather they must serve them all the more, since those who benefit by their service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these duties.
text.Rotherham: They, however, that have believing masters, let them not despise them, because they are, brethren but, the more, be doing them service, because, believing and beloved, are they who, from the good workmanship, receive advantage. These things, be teaching, and exhorting:—

text.NRSV: Whoever teaches otherwise and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that is in accordance with godliness
text.Rotherham: If anyone doth otherwise teach, and doth not adhere to healthful discourses—those of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the teaching that is, according to godliness,

text.NRSV: is conceited, understanding nothing, and has a morbid craving for controversy and for disputes about words. From these come envy, dissension, slander, base suspicions,
text.Rotherham: He is beclouded, knowing, nothing, rightly, but is diseased about questionings, and word-battles—out of which spring envy, strife, defamations, wicked surmising,

text.NRSV: and wrangling among those who are depraved in mind and bereft of the truth, imagining that godliness is a means of gain.
text.Rotherham: incessant quarrellings of men wholly corrupt in their mind and bereft of the truth,—supposing godliness to be, a means of gain!

text.NRSV: Of course, there is great gain in godliness combined with contentment;
text.Rotherham: Now it is a great means of gain—godliness, with a sufficiency of one’s own;

text.NRSV: for we brought nothing into the world, so that we can take nothing out of it;
text.Rotherham: For, nothing, brought we into the world, neither, to take anything out, are we able;—

text.NRSV: but if we have food and clothing, we will be content with these.
text.Rotherham: And, having sustenance and covering, we shall be content;

text.NRSV: But those who want to be rich fall into temptation and are trapped by many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
text.Rotherham: But, they who are determined to be rich, fall into temptation, and a snare, and many foolish and hurtful covetings, the which, sink men into ruin and destruction,—

text.NRSV: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil, and in their eagerness to be rich some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pains.
text.Rotherham: For, a root of all the vices, is the love of money, which, some, being eager for, have been seduced from the faith, and have pierced, themselves, about with many pangs.

text.NRSV: But as for you, man of God, shun all this; pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, gentleness.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, O man of God! from these things, flee! and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, meekness;

text.NRSV: Fight the good fight of the faith; take hold of the eternal life, to which you were called and for which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
text.Rotherham: Be contesting the noble contest of the faith,—lay hold of the age-abiding life—unto which thou wast called, and didst make the noble confession before many witnesses.

text.NRSV: In the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who in his testimony before Pontius Pilate made the good confession, I charge you
text.Rotherham: I charge thee, before God, who engendereth life in all things, and Christ Jesus, who, before Pontius Pilate, witnessed the noble confession,

text.NRSV: to keep the commandment without spot or blame until the manifestation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: That thou keep the commandment without spot, free from reproach, until the forthshining of our Lord Jesus Christ—

text.NRSV: which he will bring about at the right time—he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
text.Rotherham: Which, in its own fit times, the happy and only Potentate will shew—the King of them that reign, and Lord of them that wield lordship,

text.NRSV: It is he alone who has immortality and dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see; to him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Who alone hath immortality, dwelling in light unapproachable,—Whom no man hath seen—nor can see: unto whom, be honour and might age-abiding. Amen.

text.NRSV: As for those who in the present age are rich, command them not to be haughty, or to set their hopes on the uncertainty of riches, but rather on God, who richly provides us with everything for our enjoyment.
text.Rotherham: Upon them who are rich in the present age, lay thou charge—not to be high-minded, nor to have set their hope on, riches’, uncertainty,—but on God, who offereth us all things richly for enjoying,

text.NRSV: They are to do good, to be rich in good works, generous, and ready to share,
text.Rotherham: To be doing good, to be rich in noble works, to be, generous in giving, ready for fellowship,—

text.NRSV: thus storing up for themselves the treasure of a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of the life that really is life.
text.Rotherham: Treasuring up for themselves a good foundation for the future, that they may lay hold on the life [which is life] indeed.

text.NRSV: Timothy, guard what has been entrusted to you. Avoid the profane chatter and contradictions of what is falsely called knowledge;
text.Rotherham: O Timothy! that which hath been entrusted, do thou guard, avoiding the profane pratings and oppositions of falsely named knowledge,—

text.NRSV: by professing it some have missed the mark as regards the faith. Grace be with you.
text.Rotherham: Which, some professing, concerning the faith, have missed the mark! Favour be with you.
